html:
Exclusive to:

InactiveActiveItem

“"Transdermal Induction Neurological Galvanic Learning Experience" Uses electrolunetic technology to deliver shocks to conductive entities.

Once toggled, the T.I.N.G.L.E. node will search for another nearby node to link to, establishing an electric field between the two.

No physical harm is done (mental harm is up for debate). Most subjects will flee away after the brief shock.

The tester is recommended to use insulative gear to avoid this special learning experience!”

-

The T.I.N.G.L.E. Node is a structure that can link with up to 2 other T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes within a 2.5 Tile radius. To establish a connection, the player must first activate the node. Once activated, it will search for other active nodes nearby and connect to them. T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes cannot make connection when placed on Floating Platforms.

Field

Once two or more T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes are connected, they will form an electrical field that deals Shock to mobs and characters that move through it, or to mobs and player who attack it. The shock does not deal any damage. An activated T.I.N.G.L.E. Node will not shock players when they try to destroy it by Hammer.

This type of shock is different than other sources of shock and has many different effects. Other sources of shock last 0.8 seconds but this only lasts for 0.4 seconds. Shocks from T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes do not chain to nearby creatures. Also, plant-based mobs, including Wormwood, will not catch fire from being shocked by T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes.

After getting shocked, the mob will run in the opposite direction of the barrier for a short amount of time, similarly to the panic state. This does not affect Giants or electricity-immune mobs.

Having 100% Lightning protection on a mob or player can protect them from getting shocked. WX-78 can also become immune to being shocked by T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes while having an Electrification Circuit plugged in and active.

Like other sources of electricity, a shock from a T.I.N.G.L.E. Node will charge a Volt Goat.

Blueprint

After releasing the Scrappy Werepig, the T.I.N.G.L.E. Node's Rare Blueprint will be present on top of the Teetering Junk Pile.

If the Scrappy Werepig has not been defeated, the blueprint's drop chance will increase every time player rummages through the Teetering Junk Pile. On the 10th rummage, the drop is guaranteed. This allows the blueprint to be obtained without fighting the Scrappy Werepig. However, rummaging the Junk Pile will aggro the Scrappy Werepig.

If the Scrappy Werepig has been defeated, the blueprint is guaranteed to drop on the first rummage.

Trivia

  • In the beta, the T.I.N.G.L.E. Node was named "Electric Fence Post".
  • The T.I.N.G.L.E. Node's description claims that it may cause "mental harm". However, being shocked does not have any effect on the player's sanity.

InactiveActiveItem
“It is not functional as a standalone unit.”
–Wilson
“Dumb post.”
–Willow
“Little post have no friends.”
–Wolfgang
“What a lonely post.”
–Wendy
“STILL SINGLE?”
–WX-78
“The post is not conducting a current at the moment.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's just a post now.”
–Woodie
“Perhaps its best left alone.”
–Maxwell
“Alöne, it falters. Amöng its kin, it thrives.”
–Wigfrid
“It's not doing anything!”
–Webber
“This appliance does not work on its own.”
–Warly
“No friend for zap friend”
–Wormwood
“It ain't connected yet.”
–Winona
“Don't think it's linked.”
–Wortox
“Where zap? Make zap.”
–Wurt
“Don't worry, Wobes. It's not connected.”
–Walter
“Did I forget to connect this one?”
–Wanda
“Aw, it found a connection.”
–Wilson
“Better not shock me!”
–Willow
“Wolfgang not afraid of little electric tickles!”
–Wolfgang
“It fulfills its purpose in connection.”
–Wendy
“LINK ESTABLISHED!”
–WX-78
“The post is conducting a current just strong enough to deter uninsulated creatures.”
–Wickerbottom
“That'll straighten your hair!”
–Woodie
“I don't trust the power source.”
–Maxwell
“It cönjures a defensive permimeter wörthy öf Thör!”
–Wigfrid
“We're not afraid of zaps.”
–Webber
“How well does it fry?”
–Warly
“Zap friends!”
–Wormwood
“Watch your whiskers around these things!”
–Winona
“Think it's linked.”
–Wortox
“Me touch.”
–Wurt
“Stay back, Woby!”
–Walter
“Delivers an unforgettable shock.”
–Wanda
“It's not a tree, but it must be planted.”
–Wilson
“It's useless like that.”
–Willow
“Must plant little post.”
–Wolfgang
“It's no use to anyone like that.”
–Wendy
“SETUP REQUIRED”
–WX-78
“This is an electric fence post and it must be planted before it can be used.”
–Wickerbottom
“I suppose I could plant it.”
–Woodie
“I don't like it.”
–Maxwell
“Let us establish a bastiön fit för the thunder göd!”
–Wigfrid
“Where should we put it?”
–Webber
“I must find a good spot for this.”
–Warly
“Help zap friend?”
–Wormwood
“It ain't planted yet.”
–Winona
“Yet to set.”
–Wortox
“Where put for zap?”
–Wurt
“We have to plant it first.”
–Walter
“Must remember to plant this.”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

T.I.N.G.L.E. Nút là một cấu trúc có thể liên kết với tối đa 2 T.I.N.G.L.E khác. Các nút trong bán kính Ngói 2,5. Để thiết lập kết nối, trước tiên người chơi phải kích hoạt nút. Sau khi được kích hoạt, nó sẽ tìm kiếm các nút hoạt động khác gần đó và kết nối với chúng. T.I.N.G.L.E. Các nút không thể tạo kết nối khi được đặt trên Nền tảng nổi.

Nodes cannot make connection when placed on [[Floating Platform]]s. ==Field== Once two or more T.I.N.G.L.E. The shock does not deal any damage. An activated T.I.N.G.L.E. Node will not shock players when they try to destroy it by [[Hammer]]. This type of shock is different than other sources of shock and has many different effects. Other sources of shock last 0.8 seconds but this only lasts for 0.4 seconds. Shocks from T.I.N.G.L.E. Having 100% Lightning protection on a mob or player can protect them from getting shocked. [[WX-78]] can also become immune to being shocked by T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes while having an [[Electrification Circuit]] plugged in and active. Like other sources of electricity, a shock from a T.I.N.G.L.E. This allows the blueprint to be obtained without fighting the Scrappy Werepig. However, rummaging the Junk Pile will aggro the Scrappy Werepig.
